info.title: Tenant Admin API
info.description: APIs for tenant-level administration, including project management, requirement traceability configuration, and audit log retrieval. Access to these endpoints is restricted to users with Tenant Admin privileges.

accelqLoginUrl.description: The AccelQ login URL for API access.

tenantCode.description: Tenant code for identifying the organization.

ApiKey.name: api_key
ApiKey.description: API Key found under Auth Properties in ACCELQ User Profile.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all projects available within the tenant.

createdUserPid.description: PID of the user who created the project

createdTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the project was created

400.description: Invalid page number or page size

value.error: Requested page exceeds available pages.
Page size must be between 1 and 100.

403.description: Forbidden - User does not have permission

example.error: User do not have authorization to access this feature.

post.description:
Create a new project in the Tenant. Project name must adhere to the following criteria:
1. Must always start with an alphabetic character
2. Maximum length of 256 characters
3. Only letters, digits, spaces, and underscores allowed

projectName.description: Name of the new project. Validation rules: Cannot start with a digit, can only contain alphabets, spaces, underscores, and digits, max length 256 characters, must be unique within the tenant.

description.description: Description of the new project. Maximum length: 500 characters.

cloneConfigFromProjectPid.description: The project PID from which to clone configuration data. Must be an existing project PID.

assignedUsers.description: List of users to be assigned to the project. At least one user must be assigned.

userEmail.description: Login Email of the user. Must be an existing user in the system.

role.description: Role assigned to the user. Must be an existing role.

isAdmin.description: Whether to assign Project admin role to this user.

value.error: Project name validation failed: Cannot start with digit, max length 256, only alphabets, digits, spaces, and underscores allowed.
Maximum Project limit of 500 reached. Unable to create additional Projects.

403.description: Forbidden - User does not have permission to create projects

error.example: User do not have authorization to access this feature.
Project with PID 5 does not exist.
Project with the same name already exists.

value.error: User with email 'user@example.com' does not exist.
Role 'developer' does not exist.

noUserAssigned.summary: At least one user must be assigned

value.error: At least one user must be assigned to the project.

post.description: Creates a new project by cloning configuration from an existing project.

parameters.name: pjPid
parameters.description: The ID of the project to clone.

createdUserPid.description: PID of the user who created the project

createdTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the project was created

value.error: Project name validation failed: Cannot start with digit, max length 256, only alphabets, digits, spaces, and underscores allowed.
Maximum Project limit of 500 reached. Unable to create additional Projects.

example.error: A project with the name 'New Project2' already exists.

422.description: Project with specified PID not found

put.description: Updates the name and description of an existing project identified by its Pid.

parameters.name: pjPid
parameters.description: The ID of the project to update.

example.error: A project with the name 'Updated Project Name' already exists.

422.description: Project with specified PID not found.

patch.description:
This API configures Requirements Traceability Integration within the Project Configuration for a specified list of projects. It must be executed by a Tenant Admin to ensure access to all listed projects.

**Note:** Any existing Requirements Traceability configuration will be overwritten. However, currently mapped tickets/stories from these integrations will be retained.

projectNames.description: List of Project Display Names to configure Requirement Traceability

platform.description: Name of the Requirement Integration Platform

username.description: User name associated with Integration Platform

password/apiToken.description: User in Integration Platform authentication key, provide password for Basic auth else API token for Bearer auth type

connectionMode.description: Communication with your Jira instance can be routed either from the user's browser or from ACCELQ server. If your Jira server is blocked from outside access, choose the BROWSER option.

200.description: Bulk Integration completed with detailed results showing updated Projects, notFound Projects and skipped Projects With Traceability

value.error: Request Body is mandatory.
projectNames cannot be null or empty.
traceabilityConfig cannot be null.
platform cannot be null.
Accepted platforms are : [Atlassian Jira, Azure Dev Ops, Rally Cloud, Spira, Click Up].
Platform URL cannot be null or empty.
userName of integration platform cannot be null or empty.
Password or API Token of Integration platform is required.
subscriptionObjectId cannot be null or empty.
Workspace Id of Integration platform is required.
API Key/Token of Integration platform is required.
Password of Integration platform is required.
connectionMode cannot be null or empty.

error.example: Invalid user credentials or API key

403.description: Forbidden - User does not have permission to perform this operation

error.example: User does not have authorization to access this feature
Internal error occurred while integrating Project Requirement Traceability

post.description:
Fetches tenant-level audit log entries using a stateless cursor-based approach. Designed for clients that need to continuously stream or replay audit activity without missing or duplicating records.

## Call patterns

### Initial call (no `cursor`)

Omit the `cursor` field and supply optional filters. The server freezes those filters into an opaque cursor token returned as `nextCursor`.

- **Open-tail stream** (`createdAfter` only, no `createdBefore`): each subsequent poll advances the upper bound forward in real time, delivering new events as they are committed. The window from `createdAfter` to `now` must not exceed 90 days at the time of the initial call.
- **Fixed window** (`createdAfter` + `createdBefore`): the cursor exhausts once all matching rows in the range have been delivered (`hasMore: false`).
- **Start from now** (neither date supplied): stream begins at the current time, delivering only events that occur going forward.

### Subsequent calls (with `cursor`)

Pass only the `cursor` field from the previous response's `nextCursor`. Do not include any filters or dates — they are already frozen inside the cursor. To change filters, start a new stream by making a fresh initial call without a cursor.

## Polling

- `hasMore: true` — a full page was returned; call again immediately with `nextCursor` to retrieve the next page.
- `hasMore: false` — the stream is caught up; wait before polling again, then retry with `nextCursor`.
- **Always preserve `nextCursor`**, even when `data` is empty. Discarding it loses your position in the stream.

## Date rules

- Accepted format: ISO 8601 with explicit UTC offset, e.g. `2026-06-01T00:00:00Z`.
- `createdAfter` cannot be in the future.
- `createdAfter` cannot be older than 1 year (the audit log retention window).
- `createdBefore` requires `createdAfter`. If `createdBefore` is in the future it is silently clamped to `now`.
- `createdBefore` must be after `createdAfter` (after clamping).
- The window between `createdAfter` and `createdBefore` (or `now` for open tails) must not exceed 90 days.

## Ordering and safe upper bound

Results are returned in ascending order by event ID (i.e. chronological order). A 5-second lag is applied to the upper time bound so that in-flight transactions have time to commit before the cursor reaches them, preventing gaps in the stream.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination token returned as `nextCursor` from a previous response. When present, all other fields (`actorEmails`, `events`, `createdAfter`, `createdBefore`, `limit`) must be omitted — the cursor already encodes the frozen filters and position. To change filters, start a new stream without a cursor.

actorEmails.description: Filter to audit logs generated by these user email addresses. Case-insensitive. Ignored on subsequent calls (use `cursor` instead).

events.description:
Filter to specific event types. Values must match a valid event title exactly (case-insensitive). Ignored on subsequent calls (use `cursor` instead).

Valid values: `Admin Login`, `Failed Login Attempt`, `Create User`, `Delete User`, `Admin Privilege Granted`, `Admin Privilege Revoked`, `Update Authentication Method`, `Create Project`, `Update Project`, `Delete Project`, `Clone Project`, `Create Branch Project`, `Create Role`, `Update Role`, `Delete Role`, `Update Password Policy`, `Update SSO Details`, `Update User Session Timeout`, `Update Email Service`, `Update Activation Dates`, `Update Password`, `Password Reset`, `Update User License`, `Updated User Project Role`, `Terminate User Session`, `Deactivate User`, `Enable Live Chat For User`, `Disable Live Chat For User`, `Expire All User Passwords`, `Download Project Source`, `Update External Result Link Otp Pref`, `Merged Projects`, `Purge Results`, `Disable User Session Timeout`, `Rename Role`, `Enable IP Whitelisting`, `Disable IP Whitelisting`, `Update IP Whitelisting`, `User Login`, `API Key Invalid`.

createdAfter.description: Start of the date range (inclusive). ISO 8601 with UTC offset required (e.g. `2026-06-01T00:00:00Z`). Defaults to the current time if omitted. Cannot be in the future or older than 1 year. Ignored on subsequent calls (use `cursor` instead).

createdBefore.description: End of the date range (exclusive). ISO 8601 with UTC offset required (e.g. `2026-06-30T23:59:59Z`). Requires `createdAfter`. Clamped to `now` if set in the future. Must be after `createdAfter`. Omit to create an open-tail stream. Ignored on subsequent calls (use `cursor` instead).

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return per call. Clamped to the range [1, 1000]. Defaults to 100.

startFromNow.description: Omit all date and filter fields to begin a real-time tail starting from the current moment.

openTailFromDate.summary: Open-tail stream from a past date
openTailFromDate.description: Stream all events from a specific point in the past, continuing forward in real time. The initial window (createdAfter to now) must not exceed 90 days.

fixedWindow.description: Retrieve all events within a specific time range. The cursor exhausts once the range is fully delivered.

fixedWindowWithFilters.summary: Fixed window with actor and event filters
fixedWindowWithFilters.description: Narrow the stream to specific actors and event types within a date range.

nextPage.description: Pass only the nextCursor value from the previous response. No other fields should be included.

200.description: A page of audit log entries with cursor state.

data.description: Audit log entries for this page, ordered by event ID ascending (chronological order). May be empty when the stream is caught up.

eventId.description: Unique identifier of the audit log entry.

actor.description: The user who performed the action.

id.description: Internal user PID. Absent if the user no longer exists in the system.

email.description: Login email of the user who triggered the event.

displayName.description: Full name of the actor (first + last). Absent if the user no longer exists in the system.

description.description: Human-readable description of the action performed.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the event occurred.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor token encoding the current position in the stream and frozen filters. Always present, even on an empty page. Pass this as `cursor` in the next request. Never discard it.

hasMore.description: `true` if a full page was returned and more rows are immediately available — call again with `nextCursor` right away. `false` if the stream is caught up — wait before polling again.

pageWithMore.description: hasMore: true means another page is waiting. Fetch immediately.

caughtUp.summary: Stream caught up — wait before polling
caughtUp.description: hasMore: false and an empty data array means there are no new events yet. Save nextCursor and retry after a delay.

deletedActorEvent.summary: Event from a deleted user — id and displayName absent
deletedActorEvent.description: When the actor no longer exists in the system, only email is populated.

400.description: Bad Request — invalid filter value or constraint violation.

cursorWithFilters.summary: Cursor sent together with filters or dates

value.error: Do not send filters or dates with a cursor. To change filters, start a new sync without a cursor.
'createdAfter' cannot be in the future.
'createdAfter' exceeds the 1-year retention window.
Window exceeds 90 days. Provide 'createdBefore' to use an explicit range.
'createdBefore' requires 'createdAfter'.
'createdBefore' must be after 'createdAfter'.
Window exceeds 90 days. Narrow your date range.
'createdAfter' must be an ISO-8601 timestamp with UTC offset (e.g. 2026-06-23T00:00:00Z).
Invalid event: 'Unknown Event'.

403.description: Forbidden — caller does not have Tenant Admin privileges.

example.error: User do not have authorization to access this feature.
